subject: Human Resources
title: Positive Psychology in the Workplace
description: A 60-second lesson on Positive Psychology in the Workplace.

hook.text: Psychology spent a century studying what's wrong with people. Martin Seligman asked: what if we studied what's right?

buildup.text: Positive psychology studies strengths, flourishing, and wellbeing—not just fixing dysfunction but building what matters.

discovery.text: Seligman's PERMA: five wellbeing pillars—Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, and Accomplishment.

twist.text: Critics call it 'toxic positivity'—forcing optimism while ignoring real systemic problems.

climax.text: Applied well, positive psychology helps HR build strength-based development, meaningful recognition, and workplace flourishing.

punchline.text: Don't just fix weaknesses. Build on what already works.

quiz.question: What does PERMA stand for?
options: Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Accomplishment
